+## Step 6: Share the fixture across multiple tests
+
+Suites let you reuse the same fixture instance for a group of tests. Define a
+suite in a directory-level `test.yaml`; every test beneath that directory joins
+the suite automatically.
+
+```yaml
+# tests/http/test.yaml
+suite: smoke-http
+fixtures: [http]
+timeout: 45
+```
+
+The harness activates the `http` fixture once, runs each test in
+`tests/http/`—including nested directories—in lexicographic order, and tears the
+fixture down afterwards. Suites are directory-owned, so move tests into a
+sibling directory if they should stay independent or give the subdirectory a
+different suite. Per-test frontmatter cannot declare `suite`; the harness raises
+an error to avoid mixing suite and non-suite executions in the same tree.
+
+Use selectors when you only want to run the suite while iterating:
+
+```sh
+uvx tenzir-test --select suite=smoke-http
+```
+
+Running a single file from the suite still activates the shared fixture, making
+ad-hoc debugging straightforward without hand-written setup code.
+

@@ -182,6 +182,40 @@ The fixture launches `tenzir-node` from the directory that owns the test file, s
 `tenzir-node.yaml` placed next to the scenario can refer to files with relative
 paths (for example `schemas/alerts.yaml` or `../inputs/alerts.ndjson`).
 
+### Group fixture-backed tests into a suite
+
+When several tests should reuse the same fixture instance, promote the directory
+to a **suite** by setting `suite:` in its `test.yaml`. The suite runs each test
+sequentially under the shared fixtures before tearing them down.
+
+```yaml
+# tests/http/test.yaml
+suite: smoke-http
+fixtures: [http]
+timeout: 45
+```
+
+Everything under `tests/http/` (including nested directories) now belongs to the
+`smoke-http` suite. Suites are directory-owned: once you opt in, every
+descendant test participates. Move tests into a sibling directory if they should
+stay independent, or set a different suite at a deeper level. Per-test
+frontmatter cannot declare `suite`; the harness raises an error to avoid mixing
+suite and non-suite tests in the same tree.
+
+Suite members run in lexicographic order of their relative paths. The harness
+executes all suites before standalone tests and pins each suite to a single
+worker so shared fixtures activate only once. Use selectors to focus on a
+specific suite while iterating:
+
+```sh
+uvx tenzir-test --select suite=smoke-http
+```
+
+Invoking a single file from the suite (`uvx tenzir-test
+tests/http/echo-read.tql`) still activates the suite fixtures, runs that test,
+and stops the fixture immediately afterwards—handy for debugging without
+manually juggling setup and teardown.
+

+## Suites
+
+Suites let you run several tests under one shared fixture lifecycle. Declare a
+suite in a directory-level `test.yaml`; the definition applies to every test
+under that directory, including nested subdirectories.
+
+```yaml
+# tests/http/test.yaml
+suite: smoke-http
+fixtures: [http]
+timeout: 45
+```
+
+Key rules:
+
+- Suites are directory-owned. Once a `test.yaml` sets `suite`, all descendants
+  belong to that suite. Put tests that should remain independent outside the
+  suite directory or in a sibling directory with a different suite.
+- Per-test frontmatter may not declare `suite`. Mixing suite and non-suite tests
+  in the same directory tree raises a configuration error.
+- Tests inside a suite inherit the suite’s defaults (`fixtures`, `timeout`, and
+  other keys) and can still override or extend them in their own frontmatter.
+- The harness runs suite members sequentially in lexicographic order of their
+  relative paths. Each suite occupies a single worker, but different suites can
+  run in parallel when `--jobs` allows it.
+- The CLI executes all suites before any remaining standalone tests so shared
+  fixtures start and stop predictably.
+- Target a specific suite with `uvx tenzir-test --select suite=smoke-http`. The
+  selection still accepts paths, so you can combine suite filters and explicit
+  directories.
+- Running an individual test file that belongs to a suite (`tenzir-test
+tests/http/echo-read.tql`) spins up the suite fixtures, runs that single test,
+  and tears the suite down immediately.
+
